Featured Articles

Did You Know?

…that the Internet was originally created as a military communication network in the 1960s?

…that Artificial Intelligence as a field was officially founded at a 1956 Dartmouth conference?

…that Space Exploration began with the Soviet Union's Sputnik satellite in 1957?

…that Ancient Rome lasted over 1,000 years, from 753 BC to 476 AD?

…that Poland's HP-05 combat helmet meets full NATO ballistic protection standards?

About Piast Wiki

The free encyclopaedia you can rely on

← Back to Main page

Piast Wiki is a free, multilingual encyclopaedia hosted as a single-page web application. It takes its name from the Piast dynasty — the first ruling dynasty of Poland (c. 960–1370). The project aims to provide reliable, well-sourced information in as many languages as possible, with a special focus on Polish history, military affairs, and Central & Eastern European topics.

Mission

Piast Wiki's mission is to provide free, accurate knowledge to every person on the planet. Articles are written in a neutral point of view, citing verifiable sources. All text is available under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International Licence.

Language coverage

Piast Wiki supports all 193 UN-recognised state languages. Articles currently exist in English, Polish, French, German, Spanish, Italian, Arabic, Russian, Chinese, Portuguese, Turkish, Ukrainian, Japanese, Korean, Czech, Slovak, Hungarian, and Greek.

Content

Topics covered include Polish military history and equipment, diplomacy, Central and Eastern European geography, science, technology, and biographies. The encyclopaedia currently hosts articles.

Contact

For contributions, corrections, or feedback, please use the article request system or reach out via the wiki community portal. Piast Wiki is a community-driven project and welcomes editors of all backgrounds.

← Back to Main page

Privacy Policy

Last updated: 10 March 2026

1. Introduction

Piast Wiki is committed to protecting the privacy of all visitors. This Privacy Policy explains what information Piast Wiki collects, how it is used, and your rights in relation to it. By using Piast Wiki, you agree to the practices described in this policy.

2. Information We Collect

Piast Wiki is a static, client-side encyclopaedia hosted on GitHub Pages. We do not collect, store, or process any personal data such as names, email addresses, IP addresses, or browsing behaviour. No cookies are set by Piast Wiki, and no user accounts or login systems exist on this platform.

If you submit an article request through the Request Article feature, the information you enter (article title, language, and description) is stored locally in your browser only and is not transmitted to any server.

3. Third-Party Services

Piast Wiki loads images from third-party sources including Wikimedia Commons, flagcdn.com, and other publicly accessible image hosts. These services may log requests in accordance with their own privacy policies. Piast Wiki uses fonts hosted on Google Fonts, which may set cookies or collect data as described in Google's Privacy Policy.

4. GitHub Pages Hosting

This website is hosted by GitHub Pages (GitHub, Inc.), which may collect technical data such as IP addresses and browser information as part of their hosting service. Please refer to GitHub's Privacy Statement for details.

5. Children's Privacy

Piast Wiki is an educational encyclopaedia suitable for all ages. We do not knowingly collect any personal information from children under the age of 13.

6. Changes to This Policy

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Changes will be reflected by updating the "Last updated" date at the top of this page. Continued use of Piast Wiki after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.

7. Contact

If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us via the Contact page.

← Back to Main page

Contact Piast Wiki

Get in touch with the Piast Wiki editorial team

Piast Wiki welcomes contributions, corrections, and feedback from readers around the world. Please use the appropriate channel below depending on the nature of your enquiry.

📝 Article Requests

Want a new article written? Use the article request system to suggest topics and vote on existing requests.

🖼️ Image Submissions

Have a freely licensed image to contribute? Submit it through the image upload tool for editorial review.

✉️ General Enquiries

For general questions, partnerships, or editorial matters, please reach out via email.

✉ Send Email

🐛 Report an Error

Found a factual error or broken feature? Send an email and Piast Wiki will investigate promptly.

✉ Report via Email

Editorial note: Piast Wiki is an independent encyclopaedia project. All content is provided for informational and educational purposes. We strive for accuracy but cannot guarantee the completeness of all articles. Contributions are reviewed by the editorial team before publication.

Help — Getting started

Your guide to navigating and using Piast Wiki

← Back to Main page

Reading articles

Click any article title on the Main page or use All articles to browse the full index. The Random article button picks a random entry. Use the language switcher (🌐) inside an article to switch between available translations.

Searching

Use the search bar at the top to find articles by title or keyword. Results appear in real-time as you type.

Editing and contributing

To propose a new article or suggest a topic, use the Article Requests panel on the Main page. Anyone can submit requests and vote on proposals.

Accounts

To suggest a new article or topic, use the Article Requests panel on the main page.

Image licences

The majority of images on Piast Wiki are sourced from Wikimedia Commons and are available under Creative Commons licences (CC BY-SA 4.0, CC BY 4.0, or Public Domain). Some images may be sourced from official manufacturer or government websites. Each image links to its original source where available.

Keyboard shortcuts

  • Esc — Close image lightbox
  • Ctrl+F — Browser find (search within page)

Piast Wiki — Article Quality Standards

Every article on Piast Wiki is automatically assigned a quality rating based on its length, sourcing, and completeness. Clicking a badge opens this page. Ratings are updated dynamically as articles are expanded.

Stub

Stub

A stub is a very short article that provides only the most basic information about a topic. It typically lacks sections, inline citations, and any depth of coverage. Stubs are the starting point for article development and signal that significant expansion is needed.

Criteria:

  • Article body under 3,000 characters
  • Minimal or no section structure
  • Few or no inline citations
  • Missing infobox or incomplete infobox

How to improve:

Add a proper lead paragraph, at least 2–3 content sections, and a References section with verified citations. Use the Suggest edit button on the article to propose additions.

Start

Start

A Start-class article covers the basics of a topic but is incomplete in significant ways. It has some structure and sourcing but lacks the depth, breadth, or citation density expected of a well-developed encyclopaedia article.

Criteria:

  • Article body between 3,000 and 8,000 characters
  • At least 2 main sections beyond the introduction
  • Some inline citations present
  • Basic infobox present

How to improve:

Expand each section with more detail, add subsections where appropriate, ensure all major claims are cited, and add an External links section with authoritative sources.

Good article

Good Article

A Good Article is a well-written, well-sourced article that covers its subject comprehensively. It is nearly complete but may lack the polish, imagery depth, or exhaustive referencing of a Featured Article.

Criteria:

  • Article body over 8,000 characters
  • Well-structured with multiple sections and subsections
  • Consistent inline citations throughout
  • Complete infobox with all relevant fields
  • At least one image
  • Categories and navbox templates present

How to improve to Featured:

Ensure the article is comprehensive, neutral in tone, and fully cited. The lead section should summarise the article well. All images should have proper captions. Contact the editorial team to request Featured review.

Featured article

Featured Article

A Featured Article is Piast Wiki's highest quality designation. Featured articles represent the best the encyclopaedia has to offer — comprehensive, well-written, thoroughly cited, and reviewed by the editorial team. They are highlighted on the Main page.

Criteria:

  • Comprehensive coverage of the topic with no major gaps
  • Exemplary prose — clear, engaging, neutral
  • All claims supported by inline citations from reliable sources
  • No Wikipedia links in External Links — only primary or authoritative sources
  • Full infobox, table of contents, categories, and navbox
  • At least one high-quality image with correct caption
  • Available in Polish (and ideally other languages)
  • Reviewed and approved by the Piast Wiki editorial team

⭐ Featured articles are manually designated by the editorial team. If you believe an article meets these criteria, use the Suggest edit button to nominate it for Featured status.

Quality ratings on Piast Wiki are determined automatically based on article length, with Featured status assigned manually by editors. Ratings are a guide to article completeness, not a judgement of the topic's importance.

Privacy Policy

Last updated: February 2026

1. Information We Collect

Piast Wiki is a read-only encyclopaedia. We do not require registration, login, or any personal information to browse articles. We do not collect names, email addresses, or any personally identifiable information from visitors.

Like most websites, our server may log standard technical data such as IP addresses, browser type, and pages visited for security monitoring and performance analysis. These logs are not shared with third parties.

2. Cookies

Piast Wiki uses only essential functional cookies to remember your dark mode preference. We do not use tracking cookies, advertising cookies, or third-party analytics.

3. Article Requests

Article request text is stored locally in your browser session only and is not transmitted to external servers.

4. Donations

Donation processing is handled by third-party payment providers. Piast Wiki does not store payment card details.

5. Third-Party Content

Images are loaded directly from Wikimedia Commons. We do not use advertising networks or third-party trackers.

6. Changes

We may update this policy from time to time. The date at the top reflects the most recent revision.
